# agent-connector

> MCP developers: write your MCP server + hooks (and commands / skills / subagents / memory + status line) ONCE; install them across every detected AI-agent CLI in its native config dialect ‚Äî and get default, platform-independent, local-first per-tool token telemetry for the server YOUR connector declares + wraps. Agent-CLI users: with no connector, run `agent-connector usage` to read each agent CLI's own session logs read-only and see per-CLI / per-model token totals.

## What it is

Middleware that solves two recurring problems for MCP/agent-tooling developers:
1. Every agent host re-invents MCP registration + lifecycle hooks with incompatible config files, root keys, formats (JSON/JSONC/TOML/YAML/exported TS), transports, scopes, and event names.
2. No host reports per-tool token usage back to an MCP server ‚Äî so "which of my tools cost the most context?" is otherwise unanswerable.

Declare once with `defineConnector({...})`; the CLI detects installed hosts, renders each native config, installs/syncs/uninstalls, and measures your server's own token footprint locally.

Separately, anyone running an agent CLI (Claude Code / Codex / Cursor / ‚Ä¶) ‚Äî with no connector authored ‚Äî can run `agent-connector usage` to see how many tokens their agent CLIs are burning, parsed read-only from each host's own logs (per-CLI / per-model / per-session totals, never per-MCP or per-tool).

## Two audiences

agent-connector forks into two tracks. Pick yours before reading on:

- **(A) MCP developer** ‚Äî you BUILD an MCP integration. Write your server + hooks (+ commands / skills / subagents / memory + status line) once with `defineConnector()`, then deploy across the 29 registered adapters with one CLI. agent-connector wraps your stdio server in its `serve` telemetry proxy, so you get per-MCP and per-tool token counts for **YOUR own server** (the MCP your connector declares + wraps). Owns: `defineConnector`, install/uninstall/upgrade, doctor/detect/status, package, and the `telemetry` command (`--by mcp|tool|surface`).
- **(B) Agent-CLI user** ‚Äî you just USE an agent CLI and have NOT authored a connector. Your entire surface is one connector-free command: `agent-connector usage report|export|leaderboard`, which scans each agent CLI's own session logs read-only. No `defineConnector`, no install, no config file.

The one accuracy-critical boundary: if you BUILD an MCP integration, agent-connector deploys it everywhere and measures **your own server's** per-tool tokens; if you just USE agent CLIs, agent-connector reads their logs to show you **per-CLI / per-model** token totals. The connector-free `usage` path reports WHOLE-CONVERSATION totals grouped by platform | project | session | model | day ‚Äî it does NOT and cannot itemize cost by individual MCP server or tool, because agent CLIs do not log per-tool token attribution. Per-MCP and per-tool numbers come only from the serve-proxy telemetry that a developer's own connector produces (track A).

## Two pillars

- **Single-API multi-platform deployment.** One declarative + programmatic config ‚Üí adapters render it into each platform's native MCP + hook + content config; one CLI installs/syncs/uninstalls everywhere.
- **Default per-tool token telemetry for the MCP server YOUR connector declares + wraps (stdio only).** Platform-independent, local-first, privacy-preserving (aggregate counts, never content). On by default for stdio servers (remote http/sse/ws servers are registered but not wrapped, so they yield no per-tool telemetry); opt-out granular. This is the developer (track A) capability ‚Äî it measures your own server, not arbitrary MCPs an end user runs.

## Surfaces (declare once, deploy everywhere)

- `server` ‚Äî one transport-polymorphic MCP server (stdio | http | sse | ws).
- `hooks` ‚Äî normalized lifecycle hooks, 12 canonical events (SessionStart, SessionEnd, UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PreCompact, Stop, Notification, PermissionRequest, PostToolUseFailure, SubagentStart, SubagentStop).
- `commands` ‚Äî slash commands (content files).
- `skills` ‚Äî Agent Skills (SKILL.md folders + resources).
- `subagents` ‚Äî named subagents (system prompt + tool/model scoping).
- `memory` ‚Äî standing guidance written as a managed marker block into each host's memory/rules file (AGENTS.md-first).
- `statusline` ‚Äî HUD/status-line handler (SINGULAR, not an array): `render(ctx: StatuslineContext) ‚Üí string`; deploys as a host-exec command on each status refresh; FAIL-SAFE (any error ‚Üí exit 0 + empty stdout). v1: claude-code only; other adapters skip-warn.
- `actions` ‚Äî dispatch backbone: `actions?: ActionDef[]` (`{ id, description?, run(ctx: HostCtx): ActionResult|void|Promise<‚Ä¶>, hosts? }`); universal verb `agent-connector action <platform> <actionId> --connector <id>` loads and runs `run(ctx)`. User-triggered error semantics: unknown id or throw ‚Üí exit 1 + stderr (NOT fail-safe-silent). v1: dispatch backbone only; install skip-warns on every host (no host affordance emitter yet ‚Äî binding to a slash/keybinding needs generated IDE extensions, deferred).

Memory surface (the fourth content surface): declare `memory: [{ content }]` once and each supporting adapter upserts a marker-fenced, hash-stamped managed block (`<!-- agent-connector:begin <connectorId>/<name> hash=<sha256-12> -->` ‚Ä¶ `<!-- agent-connector:end <connectorId>/<name> -->`, plus a one-line do-not-edit notice) into the memory file that host actually reads. AGENTS.md-FIRST: 27 of the 29 hosts read the open AGENTS.md standard (agents.md, Linux Foundation-stewarded), so project scope targets `<projectDir>/AGENTS.md` ‚Äî write guidance once, it lands in the standard file across every adopter host. Exclusive/first-match readers are PROBED so the block lands where the host actually reads (zed's nine-candidate first-match list, warp's WARP.md priority, hermes' .hermes.md, opencode's CLAUDE.md fallback, codex's AGENTS.override.md; openclaw maps both scopes to its agent workspace). User scope: the host's documented global memory file ‚Äî AGENTS.md where one exists (e.g. `~/.codex/AGENTS.md`, `~/.config/zed/AGENTS.md`), else the host's own file (`~/.qwen/QWEN.md`, goose `.goosehints`, `~/.copilot/copilot-instructions.md`, kilo/roo/kiro rules-dir `agent-connector.md`); hosts with app/UI-managed user rules skip-warn. The two exceptions: claude-code ‚Üí CLAUDE.md (official docs: "Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md, not AGENTS.md"; HTML-comment markers are stripped from the model's context there ‚Äî invisible to Claude, still parseable by us; opt-in `memory.mode: "agents-import"` writes AGENTS.md + a managed `@AGENTS.md` import bridge in CLAUDE.md instead) and gemini-cli ‚Üí GEMINI.md (AGENTS.md only when the user's `context.fileName` setting opts in ‚Äî probed, never edited). Edits are surgical: bytes outside the marker pair are never touched; an unchanged block is an O(1) `skip`; a USER-EDITED block (actual inner hash ‚âÝ recorded hash) warns and is left intact unless `install --force` (backup first); uninstall removes memory FIRST, excising every block under the connector's marker prefix (ledger + marker scan ‚Äî works without local state), and `doctor` checks block present / hash intact / user-edited / file missing. Budgets: hard 16 KiB cap per entry, soft 4 KiB warn ‚Äî memory is injected into every prompt of every targeted host.

Native hooks passthrough (escape hatch): `platforms.<id>.nativeHooks` wires ANY host hook event outside the 12 normalized ones, keyed by the host's event name verbatim ‚Äî Claude Code ships 30 hook events, and the 18 non-normalized ones (TaskCreated/TaskCompleted, TeammateIdle, MessageDisplay, WorktreeCreate/WorktreeRemove, Elicitation/ElicitationResult, StopFailure, InstructionsLoaded, ConfigChange, CwdChanged, FileChanged, PostCompact, Setup, UserPromptExpansion, PermissionDenied, PostToolBatch) plus any future event are all reachable with zero agent-connector releases. No normalization: the handler receives the host's RAW stdin payload (`{ event, hostPlatform, sessionId, projectDir?, raw }`) and whatever it returns is the VERBATIM stdout JSON reply on exit 0 (void ‚Üí no output; fail-open; exit-2 blocking is NOT modeled in v1). Declaring a normalized event name there is a ConnectorConfigError. Only claude-code supports it today (`supportsNativeHooks`); other adapters skip-warn. An event is promoted into the normalized union once ‚â•3 hosts ship a native analog ‚Äî TaskCreated/TaskCompleted are the first candidates.

Host-config key patches (escape hatch): `platforms.<id>.configPatch` declares ownership-tracked patches of host-exclusive settings keys `extra` can't reach (e.g. Claude Code `env.CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS`) ‚Äî `{ key, value, reason, docsUrl? }` per patch, where `key` is a dotted LEAF path (segments `[A-Za-z0-9_-]+`, no array indices). Semantics are FIXED: set-if-absent, skip-warn on ANY conflict (present key, drifted value, non-object intermediate) ‚Äî never overwrite/delete/deep-merge; every skip prints the exact manual edit from the required `reason` (+ `docsUrl`). Ownership is a persisted refcounted ledger (`<dataRoot>/state/config-patches.json`); uninstall deletes a key only when the LAST owner releases it AND the value still equals what was written AND it was absent before install ‚Äî else the key stays, with a warn. Keys agent-connector models (`hooks*`, `mcpServers*`, `statusLine*` ‚Üí statusline surface) are rejected at defineConnector; the claude-code adapter hard-refuses a documented sensitive-key denylist (`permissions*`, `allowedTools*`, `apiKey*`, `env.ANTHROPIC_*`, `env.*TOKEN*`/`*KEY*`/`*SECRET*`/`*_PROXY`, auth/login keys). v1: claude-code only (`supportsConfigPatch`, settings.json at scope); other adapters skip-warn. Doctor reports per-patch ok/drifted/missing/orphaned and never auto-fixes drift. A patch graduates to a typed cross-host knob only when ‚â•3 hosts ship an analog.

## Quick start

The Quick Start forks by audience ‚Äî run the track that matches you.

### (A) MCP developer ‚Äî deploy MY MCP everywhere + measure my own per-tool tokens

```bash
# agent-connector is an SDK you depend on ‚Äî no global install required
npm install @ken-jo/agent-connector             # inside the package that holds your connector
cd my-mcp-project                    # contains agent-connector.config.mjs
npx @ken-jo/agent-connector detect              # which hosts are installed here?
npx @ken-jo/agent-connector install --dry-run   # preview every change, everywhere
npx @ken-jo/agent-connector install             # deploy across detected hosts (subset of the 29 adapters)
npx @ken-jo/agent-connector doctor              # per-platform health checks ‚Äî add --probe for a live MCP handshake (initialize ‚Üí ping ‚Üí tools/list)
npx @ken-jo/agent-connector upgrade             # day 2: re-render configs + heal the home-binary pointer (aliases: sync, update)
npx @ken-jo/agent-connector telemetry report --by tool   # per-tool footprint of YOUR wrapped server
npx @ken-jo/agent-connector uninstall           # full inverse ‚Äî removes everything install wrote (--purge clears framework state; --dry-run works here too)
```

Or ship a branded CLI: `createConnectorCli({ name, connector })` from `agent-connector/cli` exposes every subcommand under your own bin, auto-scoped to your connector (`acme-db install` ‚âà `agent-connector install --connector ./agent-connector.config.mjs`; explicit `--connector`/`--connector-id` always overrides).

### (B) Agent-CLI user ‚Äî see the token usage of the agent CLIs I use (no connector)

```bash
# zero setup: no defineConnector, no install, no config file
npx @ken-jo/agent-connector usage report                      # totals read read-only from each host's own logs
npx @ken-jo/agent-connector usage leaderboard --by platform   # which agent CLI burned the most tokens
npx @ken-jo/agent-connector usage report --by model --since 7d
npx @ken-jo/agent-connector usage export --format csv --out usage.csv
```

`usage` groups ONLY by platform | project | session | model | day ‚Äî it shows per-CLI / per-model / per-session totals, NOT per-MCP or per-tool costs (agent CLIs do not log per-tool token attribution). To get per-MCP / per-tool numbers for an MCP, that MCP must be deployed and wrapped via a connector (track A). Note: 5 'synced' platforms (cursor, antigravity, antigravity-cli, trae, warp) are reported as skipped unless a local cache already exists; some readers are host-estimated (flagged in the CONFIDENCE column).

## Package subpaths

- `.` (`@ken-jo/agent-connector`) ‚Äî root: `defineConnector`, `ConnectorConfigError`, public types (unchanged).
- `./sdk` (`@ken-jo/agent-connector/sdk`) ‚Äî consolidated authoring surface: `defineConnector` + the `define*` family (`defineCommand`, `defineSkill`, `defineSubagent`, `defineMemory`, `defineConfigPatch`, `defineNativeHook`, `defineStatusline`, `defineAction`) + `defineHook` (event-parameterized ‚Äî leading event string narrows the handler payload type, e.g. `defineHook("PreToolUse", { handler(evt){‚Ä¶} })`) + introspection (`capabilitiesOf(host)`, `hostsSupporting(surface)`, `surfaceSupport(host,surface)`, `SURFACE_PREDICATES`). All async; adapters load lazily. `SurfaceName`: server|hooks|commands|skills|subagents|memory|statusline|configPatch|nativeHooks|actions.
- `./sdk/test` (`@ken-jo/agent-connector/sdk/test`) ‚Äî offline harness: `explain(connector)` ‚Üí per-host √ó per-declared-surface matrix (`ExplainRow[]`: `{ host, surface, support:"native"|"skip-warn"|"disabled", reason }`); `simulate(connector,{surface,host,event?,input})` ‚Üí runs the REAL adapter parse‚Üíhandler‚Üíformat chain offline and reports `{ honored, hostReply?, reason }` ‚Äî encodes each host's actual honor/drop/degrade contract (e.g. codex drops `context` on UserPromptSubmit ‚Üí `honored:false`; `deny` on Stop is continuation/persistence ‚Üí `honored:true`). Answers "does my handler/HUD actually work on host X?" before touching a real host.

## Key API: defineConnector fields

- `id` (required, kebab-case), `displayName`, `version`.
- `server?: ServerDef` ‚Äî `transport`, `command`/`args`/`env`/`cwd` (stdio) or `url`/`headers`/`auth` (remote), `tools`, `timeoutMs`, `enabled`, `wrapForTelemetry`.
- `hooks?: HooksConfig` ‚Äî per event: `{ matcher?, handler(evt) }` (matcher = tool name on tool events incl. PermissionRequest/PostToolUseFailure; agent type on SubagentStart/SubagentStop); handler returns `{ decision: allow|deny|modify|context|ask, reason?, updatedInput?, additionalContext?, updatedOutput? }` or void.
- `commands?`, `skills?`, `subagents?` ‚Äî content-only surfaces (files agent-connector owns).
- `memory?: MemoryDef[]` ‚Äî `{ name? (default "memory"), description?, content }`; standing guidance ‚Üí managed marker blocks in each host's memory/rules file (AGENTS.md-first; per-host tuning via `platforms.<id>.memory: false | { path?, mode? }`, `mode` claude-code-only).
- `statusline?: StatuslineDef` ‚Äî `{ name? (default "statusline"), description?, render(ctx: StatuslineContext): string | Promise<string> }`; SINGULAR handler surface (not an array). On claude-code: install writes `settings.json.statusLine = { type: "command", command: "<homeBin> statusline claude-code --connector <id>" }` via the configPatch ownership ledger (set-if-absent, skip-warn if another owner holds it, refcounted, reversible). `StatuslineContext` provides `{ host, connectorId?, sessionId?, cwd?, model?{id?,displayName?}, cost?{totalUsd?}, context?{usedTokens?,maxTokens?,percent?}, transcriptPath?, raw }`. Runtime: FAIL-SAFE (any error ‚Üí exit 0 + empty stdout). v1: claude-code only; other adapters skip-warn. Helper: `defineStatusline({ render })` (typed identity).
- `telemetry?: TelemetryConfig` ‚Äî `enabled` (default true), `modelFamilyHint`, `measureToolDefs`, `hostNativeUsage`, `store`, `calibration`.
- `platforms?` ‚Äî per-platform overrides / escape hatch (`extra`, disable a surface, force scope, `nativeHooks` native-event passthrough, `configPatch` host-config key patches, `memory` target/mode tuning).
- `targets?` ‚Äî `"auto"` (default, all detected) or an explicit `PlatformId[]`.
- `publish?` ‚Äî distribution metadata for the official MCP artifacts (`package --format mcp-server-json|mcpb`): `registryNamespace` (reverse-DNS namespace YOU own; server.json name = `<namespace>/<id>`), `packageName` (your REAL published package), `registryBaseUrl?`, `author { name, email?, url? }`. Optional; the relevant format raises a clear error only when its required field is missing.

Validation: throws `ConnectorConfigError` on bad id, non-function handler, missing required field, duplicate surface name, skill description >1024 chars, unsafe `resources` path, or memory content >16 KiB / containing the literal managed-block marker tokens.

## CLI commands

- `detect` ‚Äî list installed platforms, scope, capabilities, hook paradigm.
- `install [--method direct|marketplace] [--scope user|project] [--targets a,b] [--connector path] [--dry-run] [--force]` ‚Äî targets the hosts DETECTED on your machine (or the explicit `--targets` / `connector.targets` you name), intersected with the 29-adapter registry; there is no "install to all 29 unconditionally" path (an empty target set just warns). `--method marketplace` (drivable: claude-code, codex, opencode, kilo, kilo-cli, antigravity, antigravity-cli live-verified on Linux+Windows+macOS; droid, qwen-code driver-shipped; gemini-cli LEGACY ‚Äî sunsetting toward Antigravity, driver kept for existing installs) drives the host's own plugin flow instead of writing config ‚Äî stage the bundle, register a local marketplace where the host has one, run the host's plugin-install verb (`claude/codex plugin install`/`add`, `gemini extensions install`, `agy plugin install`), or for npm-plugin hosts write a local `file://` entry into the config plugin array ‚Äî headless + idempotent, with a guard refusing the same connector by BOTH methods. Other marketplace-format hosts still print manual install commands. `--force` overwrites user-edited memory blocks (hash drift) after a timestamped backup; default is warn-and-leave.
- `uninstall [--method auto|direct|marketplace] [--connector-id <id>] [--targets ‚Ä¶] [--purge] [--dry-run]` ‚Äî full inverse; `--method auto` (default) reverses whichever method is actually installed; `--purge` also removes registered framework state + the home binary when no connectors remain.
- `upgrade [--channel stable|latest]` ‚Äî ONE "bring everything current" verb (aliases: `sync`, `update`): idempotent re-render (identical entries report skip), heals the stable home pointer, prints managed-update guidance (never silent).
- `doctor [--targets ‚Ä¶] [--probe] [--json]` ‚Äî per-platform health checks; `--probe` runs a live MCP handshake (initialize ‚Üí ping ‚Üí tools/list) against the real stdio server; non-zero exit on FAIL.
- `status [--json]` ‚Äî light install-state summary (which connectors are present on which hosts); always exits 0.
- `package [--format <fmt>|all] [--out <dir>]` ‚Äî emit a marketplace/extension bundle (9 host formats: claude-plugin (default) ¬∑ codex-plugin ¬∑ factory-plugin ¬∑ gemini-extension ¬∑ qwen-extension ¬∑ agy-plugin ¬∑ cursor-plugin ¬∑ kimi-plugin ¬∑ npm-plugin) or an official MCP standard artifact by explicit name: `mcp-server-json` (MCP Registry server.json) ¬∑ `mcpb` (MCPB manifest) ‚Äî both require the connector's `publish` block and are excluded from `all`.
- `telemetry report|export|leaderboard` ‚Äî **(track A, developer)** per-MCP / per-tool token telemetry for the server YOUR connector declares + wraps (`report --by tool|session|project`, `leaderboard --by mcp|tool|surface`); requires a registered connector + serve-proxy traffic + stdio transport.
- `usage report|export|leaderboard` ‚Äî **(track B, end user, connector-free)** host-native usage parsed read-only from each agent CLI's own session logs. Groups ONLY by `platform|project|session|model|day` ‚Äî per-CLI / per-model / per-session totals with NO per-MCP or per-tool dimension (agent CLIs do not log per-tool token attribution). `report --by platform|project|session|model|day`, `leaderboard --by platform|model`.
- `leaderboard` ‚Äî unified: üîå per-MCP (needs connector + serve traffic) ¬∑ üñ•Ô∏è host/user (the only board with no setup) ¬∑ üõ∞Ô∏è live host-native turns (needs the opt-in usage hook, installed only by Gemini-CLI / Antigravity); never summed across boards.
- `hook`, `serve`, `usage-event`, `statusline`, `action` ‚Äî internal entrypoints hosts point at.

## Supported platforms by paradigm (29)

- `json-stdio` (full hook dispatch, 16): claude-code, codex, cursor, vscode-copilot, jetbrains-copilot, copilot-cli, gemini-cli, qwen-code, kiro, kimi, crush, goose, hermes, droid, antigravity, antigravity-cli.
- `mcp-only` (MCP registration only, 8): warp, roo-code, trae, zed, amp, codebuff, mux, pi.
- `ts-plugin` (generated bridge module, 5): opencode, kilo-cli, kilo, omp, openclaw.

## Telemetry / leaderboards

- Default tokenizer: `gpt-tokenizer` (pure-JS); `o200k_base` for OpenAI/Codex, documented approximation for Anthropic; falls back to `chars/4` heuristic. Every row tagged `tokenizer-exact | tokenizer-calibrated | tokenizer-approx | heuristic | host-native`.
- Two axes, never summed: the developer/surface axis (üîå per-MCP serve-proxy rows ‚Äî scopes call|tool_defs|hook ‚Äî plus static command/skill/subagent context footprints; `telemetry leaderboard --by mcp|tool|surface`) vs the user/host axis (üñ•Ô∏è host CLI-log scans + üõ∞Ô∏è live host-native model_turn rows).
- Privacy: aggregate counts only; zero egress by default. Opt out: `AGENT_CONNECTOR_TELEMETRY=0` or `telemetry: { enabled: false }`.
- Env switches: `AGENT_CONNECTOR_DATA_DIR` (relocate data-root) ¬∑ `AGENT_CONNECTOR_TELEMETRY=0` (kill switch) ¬∑ `AGENT_CONNECTOR_HOST_NATIVE=1` (force host-native turn capture) ¬∑ `AGENT_CONNECTOR_CALIBRATE=anthropic` + `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (BOTH required to enable the opt-in count_tokens calibration sampler).

## Extensibility

- Adding a platform = one lazily-loaded registry entry (`src/adapters/registry.ts`) + one adapter class; `platforms.<id>.extra` is the per-platform escape hatch (thin universal core, fat per-adapter tail).

## Links

- [README](README.md) ‚Äî overview + quick start.
- [Architecture](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) ‚Äî the authoritative design.
- [Full LLM reference](llms-full.txt) ‚Äî exhaustive API + CLI + platform + telemetry reference.
- [Example connector](examples/acme-db/agent-connector.config.mjs).
- [Skill](skills/agent-connector/SKILL.md) ‚Äî agent-facing how-to (deployable via the skills surface).
